name: strawberry peach caprese
instructions:
Make the strawberry peach vinaigrette per its own recipe.

Build the salad: arrange a bed of mixed greens (spring mix). Tear fresh mozzarella into chunks and scatter over the greens. Top with the strawberry peach vinaigrette — spoon it on rather than pour it (the dressing is pulpy and thick by design).

Finish with toasted walnuts for crunch.
notes:
The sweet-fruit cousin of caprese salad. Same structural moves as the standard caprese: leafy greens + soft fresh cheese + a fruit-forward dressing that does the heavy flavor lifting. Where the standard caprese uses tomato vinaigrette, this version uses the strawberry peach vinaigrette.

CHEESE FORK: mozzarella is the canonical caprese cheese and the default here. Goat cheese and brie are valid alternatives and each pull the salad in a different direction:
- MOZZARELLA: cleanest, most caprese-traditional, lets the fruit lead.
- GOAT CHEESE: sharper, tangier; works especially well alongside the walnuts.
- BRIE: richer and creamier; suits a more substantial salad.
Pick one — layering all three reads busy.

TOASTED WALNUTS, NOT RAW: toasting brings out the nutty depth that balances the sweet fruit dressing. Raw walnuts read tannic against the sweetness. A quick dry-toast in a pan for a few minutes is enough.

WHY SPRING MIX (MIXED GREENS): the salad needs a green base that brings bitterness/structure but stays out of the dressing's way. Per the herbs and greens article, mixed greens (baby arugula mix) are the right call — calibrated bitterness without overpowering. Romaine or iceberg would read too neutral; full-grown arugula would compete with the dressing.
